info.title: K8S Cloud Gateway
info.description: Integrated Framework for Multi-Cluster Kubernetes Governance

post.description:
Receives the cluster ID and the requested profile.
If the password is correct, returns a JWT that wraps
the K8s credentials for that specific profile.

get.description:
Checks cluster connectivity via /version (no RBAC permissions required).
Used by the frontend after login before displaying the dashboard.

post.description: Creates a new namespace in the cluster.

delete.description:
Removes a namespace from the cluster.
Warning: this operation is destructive for all resources inside the namespace.

get.description:
Lists the namespaces in the cluster.
Always returns HTTP 200: the 'can_list' field indicates whether the profile
has permission to list (False = access denied, empty list).
Lists the ConfigMaps in the specified namespace.
Lists the Secrets in the specified namespace (name, type and keys only — values are never exposed).
Lists Kubernetes events in the namespace, sorted by descending timestamp.

Lists the Pods in the namespace.
Supports optional label filtering via query string
(e.g. ?label_selector=app=nginx).
Returns the details of a single Pod.

delete.description: Deletes a Pod. Kubernetes automatically recreates the Pod if it is managed by a controller.

get.description:
Returns the last N log lines of a Pod as plain text.
The `tail` parameter controls how many lines are returned (default 100, max 5000).

parameters.name: tail

schema.description: Number of log lines to return (1-5000)
schema.title: Tail

parameters.description: Number of log lines to return (1-5000)

get.description:
Lists the Deployments in the namespace.
Supports optional label filtering via query string.
Returns the details of a single Deployment, including replicas and image.

delete.description: Deletes a Deployment from the namespace.

patch.description:
Scales a Deployment to the specified number of replicas.
Passing replicas=0 suspends the deployment without deleting it.

parameters.name: replicas

schema.description: Desired number of replicas (>= 0)
schema.title: Replicas

parameters.description: Desired number of replicas (>= 0)

post.description:
Performs a rollout restart of the Deployment by injecting a timestamp annotation.
Equivalent to `kubectl rollout restart deployment/<name>`.

get.description: Lists the Services in the namespace with type, ClusterIP and creation timestamp.
Returns the details of a Service, including ports and selectors.

delete.description: Deletes a Service from the namespace.

post.description:
Applies a multi-resource YAML manifest independently.
The namespace is read directly from the YAML file.
If absent, K8s will use the default or return an error based on permissions.

delete.description: Deletes a ConfigMap from the namespace.
Deletes a Secret from the namespace.

get.description:
Returns technical details of the cluster nodes: CPU, memory,
kubelet version, OS and role (Control Plane / Worker).
Requires Cluster Admin permissions on the Service Account.

schema.title: Response Get Cluster Nodes Api V1 Cluster Nodes Get

get.description: Lists the ServiceAccounts in the namespace.

delete.description: Deletes a ServiceAccount from the namespace.

get.description: Lists the Roles in the namespace with the number of associated rules.

delete.description: Deletes a Role from the namespace.

get.description: Lists the RoleBindings in the namespace with the associated role and subjects.

delete.description: Deletes a RoleBinding from the namespace.

get.description: Lists the Ingress resources in the namespace with host, addresses and creation timestamp.

delete.description: Deletes an Ingress resource from the namespace.

get.description: Cluster-level resource: PersistentVolumes (PV).
Namespace-level resource: PersistentVolumeClaims (PVC).
Lists all StorageClasses available at cluster level.
Retrieves the StatefulSets, optionally filtered by label.

delete.description: Deletes a specific StorageClass at cluster level.

get.description:
Returns the detailed status, manifest and notes of a release.
HTTP 400 if the release does not exist.

Returns the revision history of a release.
HTTP 400 if the release does not exist.

parameters.name: max

schema.description: Maximum number of revisions to return
schema.title: Max

parameters.description: Maximum number of revisions to return

get.description:
Returns the values applied to the release.
With all=false: only the override values (those passed with -f or --set).
With all=true: all values, including the chart defaults.

parameters.name: all

schema.description: If true, also includes the chart default values
schema.title: All

parameters.description: If true, also includes the chart default values

post.description:
Installs or upgrades a Helm release (`helm upgrade --install`).
If the release does not exist it is created; if it exists it is upgraded.

parameters.name: chart_ref

schema.description: Chart reference: 'repo/chart' (e.g. bitnami/nginx), local path, OCI URL
schema.title: Chart Ref

parameters.description: Chart reference: 'repo/chart' (e.g. bitnami/nginx), local path, OCI URL

parameters.name: version

schema.description: Specific chart version. If omitted, the latest is used.
schema.title: Version

parameters.description: Specific chart version. If omitted, the latest is used.

parameters.name: create_namespace

schema.description: The namespace is retrieved. If it does not exist it must be created by a SA that has the permission and not independently.
schema.title: Create Namespace

parameters.description: The namespace is retrieved. If it does not exist it must be created by a SA that has the permission and not independently.

parameters.name: atomic

schema.description: Automatic rollback if the deployment fails (--atomic)
schema.title: Atomic

parameters.description: Automatic rollback if the deployment fails (--atomic)

parameters.name: wait

schema.description: Waits for all resources to be Ready before responding (--wait)
schema.title: Wait

parameters.description: Waits for all resources to be Ready before responding (--wait)

parameters.name: timeout_seconds

schema.description: Timeout for --wait/--atomic in seconds
schema.title: Timeout Seconds

parameters.description: Timeout for --wait/--atomic in seconds

schema.description: Override values (equivalent to -f values.yaml)
schema.title: Values

delete.description:
Removes a Helm release and all K8s resources managed by it.
With keep_history=true the history is preserved to allow rollbacks.

parameters.name: keep_history

schema.description: If true, preserves the release history for future rollbacks
schema.title: Keep History

parameters.description: If true, preserves the release history for future rollbacks

post.description:
Installs a chart from a ZIP archive.
The ZIP file must contain a directory with `Chart.yaml`.
Runs `helm upgrade --install` on the extracted chart.

parameters.name: values_json

schema.description: Override values as a JSON string (e.g. '{"replicaCount": 2}')
schema.title: Values Json

parameters.description: Override values as a JSON string (e.g. '{"replicaCount": 2}')

post.description:
Rolls back a release to a specific revision.
`revision=0` is equivalent to reverting to the previous revision.

parameters.name: revision

schema.description: Target revision. 0 = previous revision (native Helm behaviour)
schema.title: Revision

parameters.description: Target revision. 0 = previous revision (native Helm behaviour)

get.description:
Lists the configured Helm repositories.
Returns { "success": true, "data": [] } if no repository is configured
(helm repo list returns rc=1 in that case: normalised in the manager).

post.description:
Adds a Helm repository.
Supports authentication for private repositories (GitLab, GitHub, etc).

parameters.name: name

schema.description: Local name of the repository
schema.title: Name

parameters.description: Local name of the repository

parameters.name: url

schema.description: URL of the Helm repository
schema.title: Url

parameters.description: URL of the Helm repository

parameters.name: username

schema.description: Optional username (e.g. 'gitlab-token')
schema.title: Username

parameters.description: Optional username (e.g. 'gitlab-token')

parameters.name: password

schema.description: Optional token/password
schema.title: Password

parameters.description: Optional token/password

post.description:
Updates the local index of repositories.
If a name is provided, updates only that repository; otherwise updates all of them.

parameters.name: name

schema.description: Optional name of the specific repository to update
schema.title: Name

parameters.description: Optional name of the specific repository to update

delete.description:
Removes a Helm repository by name (`helm repo remove`).

Not exposed in the frontend — available via /docs.
HTTP 404 if the repository does not exist.

get.description:
Searches for charts in the configured repositories (helm search repo).
HTTP 400 if no repository is configured (helm returns an error).
Empty list if no chart matches the query.

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search term (e.g. 'nginx', 'bitnami/redis')
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Search term (e.g. 'nginx', 'bitnami/redis')

parameters.name: versions

schema.description: Show all available versions of the chart
schema.title: Versions

parameters.description: Show all available versions of the chart

get.description:
Shows the default values of a chart (helm show values).
Returns raw YAML in `stdout` — not JSON.
Used by the frontend to display configurable parameters before deployment.

parameters.name: chart_ref

schema.description: Chart reference (e.g. 'bitnami/nginx', 'oci://registry/chart')
schema.title: Chart Ref

parameters.description: Chart reference (e.g. 'bitnami/nginx', 'oci://registry/chart')

parameters.name: version

schema.description: Specific version. If omitted, the latest is used.
schema.title: Version

parameters.description: Specific version. If omitted, the latest is used.

get.description:
Runs helm lint on a chart from a repository.
Always returns HTTP 200 with has_errors/has_warnings in the body —
does not raise an exception on lint failure (it is a result, not a system error).

parameters.name: chart_ref

schema.description: Chart reference (e.g. 'bitnami/nginx')
schema.title: Chart Ref

parameters.description: Chart reference (e.g. 'bitnami/nginx')

parameters.name: strict

schema.description: Treat warnings as errors
schema.title: Strict

parameters.description: Treat warnings as errors

post.description:
Runs helm lint on a chart provided as a ZIP archive.
Useful for validation before running install_from_zip.

parameters.name: values_json

schema.description: Override values as a JSON string
schema.title: Values Json

parameters.description: Override values as a JSON string

post.description: Registers a new cluster in the gateway. Accepts the cluster ID, display name, API server host and an optional CA certificate file.

get.description: Returns the list of clusters registered in the gateway. Supports optional filtering by name via the search query parameter.

post.description: Creates a new access profile associated with a cluster. Each profile holds a gateway password and a K8s service account token.

get.description: Returns the list of access profiles. Supports optional filtering by cluster_id and/or profile name.

delete.description: Removes a cluster and all its associated profiles from the gateway.

patch.description: Updates one or more fields of a registered cluster (name, host, CA certificate). Only provided fields are updated.

delete.description: Removes an access profile from the gateway.

patch.description: Updates one or more fields of an access profile (name, cluster_id, gateway_password, k8s_token). Only provided fields are updated.

get.description:
Returns the complete catalogue of audit rules defined in the registry.

Does not depend on the DB or per-cluster configuration — it is the static
catalogue of rules implemented in code. Used by the frontend to build
the configuration UI.

Response
--------
List of objects with: id, name, description, severity, needs.

get.summary: Audit rules configuration for a cluster
get.description:
Returns the configuration of all rules for the specified cluster.

For each rule includes:
- `enabled`: True if the rule is active (default) or has been explicitly
  enabled; False if it has been disabled by the admin.
- `note`: optional reason for the disablement.

Rules without explicit configuration in the DB appear with
`enabled=True` (default-on logic).

Parameters
----------
cluster_id : str
    Cluster ID (e.g. "K3S", "DIPI-1"). Case-insensitive.

patch.summary: Enable or disable a rule for a cluster
patch.description:
Enables or disables a single audit rule for a cluster (upsert).

If no record exists for (cluster_id, rule_id) it is created.
If one already exists it is updated. The `note` field is optional — if None
the previous value is preserved only when the record already existed;
for a new record it is set to None.

Parameters
----------
cluster_id : str
    Target cluster ID.
rule_id : str
    ID of the rule to configure. Must exist in the registry.
enabled : bool
    Desired state of the rule.
note : str | None
    Optional reason for the configuration.

Raises
------
HTTPException 404
    If `rule_id` does not exist in the registry.

post.summary: Reset the default configuration for a cluster
post.description:
Removes all explicit rule configuration for the cluster,
resetting every rule to its default state (enabled=True).

This does not delete the cluster or its profiles — it only removes
the records in the `audit_rule_configs` table for this cluster.

Useful when:
- A cluster is reconfigured and you want to start from scratch.
- The configuration has become inconsistent and needs to be restored.

Parameters
----------
cluster_id : str
    ID of the cluster to reset.

get.summary: Run audit on all fleet clusters
get.description:
Runs the audit rules on all registered clusters using the
FleetManager cache data.

If the cache is empty (first start, container just restarted) it forces
a synchronous fleet scan before proceeding with the audit.
In this case the response may take a few seconds.

Disabled rules for a cluster are not executed (respects the
configuration in `audit_rule_configs`).

Offline clusters receive only the `cluster-reachable` rule,
avoiding false negatives from unavailable data.

Response
--------
List of per-cluster results with: cluster_id, cluster_name, status,
score (passed rules), total (evaluated rules), score_pct, findings.

get.summary: Run audit on a single cluster
get.description:
Runs the audit rules on a single cluster.

Filters the cache data for the specified cluster.
If the cluster is not found in the cache, forces a full scan.

Parameters
----------
cluster_id : str
    Cluster ID (e.g. "DIPI-1").

Raises
------
HTTPException 404
    If the cluster is not found in the DB or in the cache.

post.summary: Force a fleet scan and return fresh audit results
post.description:
Forces a complete fleet scan, updates the FleetManager cache
and returns audit results based on fresh data.

Unlike GET /results (which uses the cache), this route
always performs real calls to the K8s clusters. It may take
several seconds depending on the number of clusters and network latency.

Side effect: updates the global FleetManager cache, also affecting
subsequent calls to GET /fleet/status and GET /results.

file.title: File
file.description: ZIP archive containing the Helm chart (must contain Chart.yaml)

file.title: File
file.description: ZIP archive containing the Helm chart

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: True to enable, False to disable

note.title: Note
note.description: Optional reason (e.g. 'development cluster — privileged pods accepted')
